pattern: Partitioning by a precomputed primality sieve on indices turns the difference of two group sums into a single weighted pass over the array.
hints: The split depends only on whether each index is prime, not on the values themselves.
Add an element to one running total when its index is prime and to the other otherwise.
Sieve primality up to the array length, sum prime-index and non-prime-index elements separately, and return the absolute difference.

edge_inputs.description: only one prime index in a short array
two prime indices including zero-valued element
single element has no prime index
max-magnitude values stress the running sums
all equal so difference depends purely on counts

stress.description: large random array over the full value range to exercise the sieve and sums
stress.note: sums can exceed 32-bit so both languages must use wide integers
